sources: WebSearch + WebFetch over public platform docs and quant-finance references. Every claim is cited in patterns_found[].source.
vocabulary_note: All entity names reference disambiguated.txt only. No new entities introduced.

platform_adapters.auth_model: api-key-optional (RSA PKCS#8 2048-bit signatures for write; read endpoints public). Headers: KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE, KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP.

rate_limits.throttle_behaviour: 429 with Retry-After header; caller must honour exponential backoff

platform_adapters.current_price_endpoint: GET /markets/{ticker}/orderbook — top-of-book yields yes-bid/yes-ask; market-price = mid
platform_adapters.price_history_endpoint: GET /markets/{ticker}/history — historical tick series retrievable retrospectively
platform_adapters.data_format: REST JSON + websocket channels (ticker, trade, orderbook_delta, market_lifecycle_v2). Public websocket channels need no auth.
platform_adapters.market-side_encoding: each market is paired binary; yes-side and no-side are complementary. market-price reported as integer cents 0-100 (normalise to 0.00-1.00 decimal per disambiguated.txt).
platform_adapters.market-resolution_mapping: status field → yes-resolved | no-resolved | cancelled | open
platform_adapters.historical_available: yes — candle/history endpoint returns retrospective ticks; no need to record live
known_quirks: prices quoted as integer cents (1-99), never 0 or 100
market-volume-24h must be computed from trades feed; no direct 24h field on all tickers
paired yes/no means NO price = 100 - YES price by construction (no independent NO order book)
auth uses RSA key pair, not simple api key — adapter must sign requests even if reading private endpoints

patterns_found.pattern: four-tier read/write quotas; Basic = 20 reads/sec, 10 writes/sec
base URL + websocket URL + endpoint shape reference
public channels: ticker, trade, orderbook_delta, market_lifecycle_v2

pitfalls.description: Integer-cent prices collide with disambiguated market-price type (0.00-1.00 decimal)
pitfalls.mitigation: adapter normalises cents/100 on ingest; schema stores decimal

pitfalls.description: RSA signing is heavyweight for purely-read adapter
pitfalls.mitigation: run adapter in api-key-optional mode; fall back to unsigned public reads

pitfalls.description: Rate limit 429 with Retry-After must be respected or adapter-status flips to rate-limited
pitfalls.mitigation: token bucket at 20 reads/sec minus safety margin; surface rate-limited pill

platform_adapters.auth_model: none-for-read. Gamma + CLOB read endpoints are public. Write endpoints (unused here — paper trades only) require L2 HMAC-SHA256 headers signed with an EOA wallet key.

rate_limits.gamma_unauthenticated: ~60 requests/min (per pm.wiki) / 4,000 per 10s (per agentbets.ai). Use the conservative 60/min value until empirically tuned.

platform_adapters.data_format: REST JSON + CLOB websocket (market, user channels). YES and NO outcomes are two distinct ERC-1155 tokens, each has its own token_id and its own order book.
platform_adapters.market-side_encoding: each binary market has two independent token_ids — one for yes, one for no. Their prices theoretically sum to 1.00 but can diverge (arbitrage signal, see STR-02).
platform_adapters.market-resolution_mapping: gamma market object fields: active, closed, resolvedBy → yes-resolved | no-resolved | cancelled | open
platform_adapters.historical_available: yes — prices-history endpoint returns full retrospective series at configurable intervals
known_quirks: CLOB — two separate token IDs per market means the yes/no order books are INDEPENDENT, unlike kalshi
price history requires token_id, not market_id — adapter must resolve market→tokens[] first
gamma.markets returns event hierarchy (Series → Events → Markets); normalise to flat market entities
enableOrderBook flag must be true before a market is tradable; otherwise only ui-priced

patterns_found.pattern: two outcome tokens per market, each redeemable for $1
base URLs for gamma, clob, data; websocket URL
prices-history endpoint shape + interval parameter
official python client handles L2 HMAC signing — adapter can import rather than reimplement if auth becomes needed

pitfalls.description: rate limit figures differ between sources (60/min vs 4000/10s)
pitfalls.mitigation: start conservative at 60/min, widen after live metrics; expose adapter-status pill

pitfalls.description: yes-token-price + no-token-price ≠ 1.00 always
pitfalls.mitigation: schema stores both tokens' market-prices separately; do not derive NO from YES

pitfalls.description: websocket limit of 5 connections per IP
pitfalls.mitigation: single shared connection multiplexing all market subscriptions in the adapter

platform_adapters.auth_model: api-key-optional. Read endpoints are public; authenticated requests use header Authorization: Key {key} or Authorization: Bearer {jwt}.
platform_adapters.list_markets_endpoint: GET /v0/markets?limit=1000 (ordered by creation date; paginate with before cursor)
platform_adapters.current_price_endpoint: GET /v0/market/{marketId} — BINARY markets expose a probability field 0-1 directly
platform_adapters.price_history_endpoint: NOT AVAILABLE as single endpoint. Bets endpoint GET /v0/bets?contractId={id} returns full bet history from which price-history must be RECONSTRUCTED retrospectively.
platform_adapters.data_format: REST JSON + websocket (topics: global/new-bet, contract/{marketId}, requires 30-60s ping)
platform_adapters.market-side_encoding: BINARY markets have single probability 0-1 (yes-price directly; no-price = 1 - yes-price, unlike polymarket). Also supports MULTIPLE_CHOICE, PSEUDO_NUMERIC, POLL, BOUNTIED_QUESTION, FREE_RESPONSE.
platform_adapters.market-resolution_mapping: isResolved (bool), resolution (outcome string), resolutionTime (epoch ms) → yes-resolved | no-resolved | cancelled | open
platform_adapters.historical_available: partial — must be stitched from /v0/bets endpoint; each bet carries probBefore/probAfter which reconstructs the series. Requires the bet-stream-replay pattern.
known_quirks: multiple market-type variants beyond binary — adapter must filter to BINARY for the four-platform unified schema (or handle PSEUDO_NUMERIC via probability mapping)
no direct price history — must reconstruct from bets
uses mana (manifold's virtual currency) not usd; market-price is still the 0-1 probability so usd mapping happens at paper-fill time, not ingest
websocket requires keepalive ping every 30-60s or connection drops

patterns_found.pattern: 500 req/min per IP; Key/Bearer auth; websocket with 30-60s ping
/v0/markets pagination; /v0/bets for trade history

pitfalls.description: price-history must be reconstructed — O(bets) work
pitfalls.mitigation: on first sync, page through bets; cache locally; subsequent syncs incremental via createdTime filter

pitfalls.description: non-binary market types could leak into unified schema
pitfalls.mitigation: adapter filter: only ingest where outcomeType === 'BINARY' (optionally PSEUDO_NUMERIC with explicit flag)

pitfalls.description: mana vs usd unit confusion for volume fields
pitfalls.mitigation: market-volume-24h stays in mana for manifold; equity mapping uses probability only

platform_adapters.auth_model: none-for-read. There is NO trading api at all (PredictIt has no programmatic trading).
platform_adapters.data_refresh: sixty seconds — the upstream data itself refreshes every 60s regardless of poll frequency
platform_adapters.price_history_endpoint: NOT AVAILABLE via api. Historical data exists only as CSV downloads from the website. Live recording is the ONLY way to build stored-price-history going forward.
platform_adapters.market-side_encoding: each market contains one or more contracts. Each contract has paired yes/no quotes (four prices: bestBuyYes, bestSellYes, bestBuyNo, bestSellNo). Shares not contracts — size-1 unit is one share paying $1 at resolution.
platform_adapters.market-resolution_mapping: status field → yes-resolved | no-resolved | cancelled | open (exact enum differs; adapter normalises)
platform_adapters.historical_available: NO — must be recorded live by the live-poll loop. This is the BINDING CONSTRAINT on backtest date-range for predictit.
known_quirks: upstream refreshes every 60s, so polling faster than 1/60s yields no new data
no websocket, no trading api, no historical bulk api
prices are in cents not dollars in some fields — verify per-field
multi-contract markets (e.g. 'who wins the nomination?' has one contract per candidate)
rate-limit is 1 req/sec — plan adapter poll cadence carefully across all markets
data-license requires attribution on any UI surface that shows predictit numbers (non-commercial use)

patterns_found.pattern: single /all/ endpoint returns every active market+contract
60-second refresh + 1 req/sec limit + attribution requirement

pitfalls.description: no historical api means backtests on predictit only cover dates since this application started recording
pitfalls.mitigation: open-question OQ-01; user must understand this constraint. Adapter writes every live tick to stored-price-history.

pitfalls.description: multi-contract markets break the one-market/one-binary assumption
pitfalls.mitigation: adapter flattens: each contract becomes its own market in the normalised schema with a contract-scoped market-id

pitfalls.description: attribution requirement
pitfalls.mitigation: market detail screen shows platform-chip badge which already reads PredictIt

engine_patterns.rationale: The same strategy code must run in both execute-backtest AND live-paper-trading-session — disambiguated.txt has strategy.decide(market-state) → orders as a single contract. An event-driven replay that emits market-data-events in chronological order allows the SAME strategy class to be called in both modes, eliminating the look-ahead bias that vectorised backtests commonly suffer from.
components: data-handler — reads stored-price-history OR live-price-feed and emits market-data-event
event-queue — FIFO of market-data-event | signal-event | order | paper-fill
strategy-runner — calls strategy.decide(market-state) when market-data-event arrives; produces zero or more orders
execution-handler — applies slippage-model to each order and emits paper-fill
portfolio-bookkeeper — updates paper-position, equity, realised-pnl, unrealised-pnl on each paper-fill
metrics-recorder — appends to equity-curve-series and computes rolling sharpe-ratio, brier-score, win-rate
engine_patterns.slippage_model_implementation: per slippage-model in disambiguated.txt: default = mid-price ± 1¢ on the aggressor side (buy fills above mid, sell fills below mid). Fallback = top-of-book-walk when order size > best-level-size: walk the book level-by-level, consuming quantity at each price, weighted-average for final fill price.
engine_patterns.order_book_crossing: if order.size ≤ bbo-size: fill at bbo-price ± 1¢; else: walk levels, accumulate filled-quantity at each price * size until order.size satisfied; fill-price = volume-weighted average. Disambiguated.txt says 'top-of-book walk' — take that literally.
simulation_vs_reality_line: SIMULATED: all paper-fills, all order fills, all portfolio state — per A11 'nothing reaches real trading APIs'
REAL: market-price data itself (live-price-feed or stored-price-history both come from actual platform APIs)
REAL: market-resolution outcomes (need to read resolved field from adapter to score brier-score)
SIMULATED: liquidity assumption — assume the paper-fill could have actually executed at the shown price+slippage; no impact on the real market

patterns_found.pattern: six-layer event-driven architecture: DataHandler, EventQueue, Strategy, Portfolio, ExecutionHandler, Performance
event-driven backtester enables code reuse between backtest and live with minimal changes
vectorised backtest is faster but enables look-ahead bugs; event-driven is slower but realistic
python reference implementation with FillEvent that carries slippage + commission

pitfalls.description: look-ahead bias if the engine passes FUTURE price ticks to strategy.decide
pitfalls.mitigation: event queue strictly time-ordered; strategy receives only market-state-at-time-t, never t+1

pitfalls.description: replay speed for multi-year backtests can be slow
pitfalls.mitigation: single-threaded event loop is fine at prediction-market tick density (far lower than hft); only optimise if measurement shows need

pitfalls.description: live mode drift vs backtest mode — two slightly different code paths diverge over time
pitfalls.mitigation: one engine class with a data-handler polymorphism; backtest-data-handler and live-data-handler both emit identical market-data-event shape

metrics_formulas.prediction_market_adaptation: In prediction markets, market-price IS a forecast probability. For each closed paper-position, forecast = market-price at time of entry; realised = 1 if market-resolution == yes-resolved and side was yes (or no-resolved and side was no), else 0. N = total closed paper-positions.
metrics_formulas.interpretation: 0 = perfect calibration; 0.25 = coin-flip (random); 1 = always certain and always wrong. Per disambiguated.txt brier-score entry.

patterns_found.pattern: mean squared error between predicted probability and outcome 0/1
brier on prediction-market prices directly — market-price acts as the forecast

pitfalls.description: which price to use as the forecast — entry price, average holding price, or price at close?
pitfalls.mitigation: OQ-02 in open_questions. RECOMMENDED: entry price (most common in trading literature; captures the decision point)

pitfalls.description: positions on markets that resolve CANCELLED have no outcome 0/1
pitfalls.mitigation: OQ-03 in open_questions. RECOMMENDED: exclude from denominator N

metrics_formulas.recommended_convention: daily returns computed on equity curve; annualisation_factor = 252 trading days (standard trading-asset convention). risk-free-rate default = 0 for paper trading (user cannot actually earn risk-free interest on simulated-bankroll).
metrics_formulas.daily_return_definition: equity[t] / equity[t-1] - 1, sampled at day boundaries from equity-curve-series

patterns_found.pattern: formula with annualisation √N where N = sampling periods per year
252 trading days is the conventional annualisation factor

pitfalls.description: prediction markets run 24/7 on some platforms (polymarket, manifold) but kalshi follows US trading hours
pitfalls.mitigation: use 365 OR 252 consistently — recommend 252 as default to match classic finance; expose as config

pitfalls.description: stdev undefined for N=1 return
pitfalls.mitigation: sharpe-ratio returns null (tile shows '—') until ≥2 returns observed

pitfalls.description: risk-free-rate assumption
pitfalls.mitigation: default 0; disambiguated.txt definition is agnostic

metrics_formulas.downside_deviation_formula: sqrt( (1/N) * Σ min(0, daily_return_i - target_return)^2 ) — only returns BELOW target contribute
metrics_formulas.recommended_convention: target_return = 0 (Minimum Acceptable Return = 0, i.e. do not lose money); same √252 annualisation as sharpe for consistency

patterns_found.pattern: downside deviation uses only returns < target, zero for returns ≥ target
CFA/GIPS canonical formulation

pitfalls.description: denominator can be zero if no negative returns yet
pitfalls.mitigation: return null (tile '—') until ≥1 downside return

pitfalls.description: target_return often set to risk-free rate, not zero
pitfalls.mitigation: keep zero default to match sharpe risk-free-rate=0 default; expose as config

metrics_formulas.algorithm: for each point in equity-curve-series, track running_peak = max(running_peak, equity[t]); drawdown[t] = (running_peak - equity[t]) / running_peak; max-drawdown = max(drawdown[]). Express as percent (positive number).
metrics_formulas.python_canonical: running_max = np.maximum.accumulate(equity); drawdowns = (running_max - equity) / running_max; mdd = drawdowns.max()

patterns_found.pattern: cumulative-max-based drawdown computation
np.maximum.accumulate pattern

pitfalls.description: percent vs dollar drawdown ambiguity
pitfalls.mitigation: disambiguated.txt already specifies percent; Portfolio tile labels 'max drawdown %'

pitfalls.description: running_peak starts at simulated-bankroll; drawdown before first gain is always 0
pitfalls.mitigation: initialise running_peak = equity[0] = simulated-bankroll

metrics_formulas.scope: computed only over paper-positions whose market-resolution ∈ {yes-resolved, no-resolved}. Open positions excluded. Cancelled positions excluded.
metrics_formulas.tie_handling: realised-pnl exactly 0 on a position is counted as a LOSS (conservative; matches common trading convention)

patterns_found.pattern: win-rate is trivial arithmetic; care is in what counts as 'closed'

pitfalls.description: positions closed by user manual exit before market-resolution — are they 'closed'?
pitfalls.mitigation: YES — any paper-position with zero net size is closed. realised-pnl comparison > 0 determines win

pitfalls.description: cancelled markets
pitfalls.mitigation: OQ-03 — recommend excluding from both numerator and denominator

strategy_specs.formula_binary_payoff: f* = (p - market_price) / (1 - market_price) for BUY YES where p = strategy's estimated true probability, market_price = current-yes-price. Derived from classic Kelly f = (bp - q)/b with b = (1 - market_price)/market_price for 0/1 payoff.
strategy_specs.position_size_usd: kelly_fraction * f* * strategy-assigned-capital, where kelly_fraction is the parameter (0 < kelly_fraction ≤ 1, typical 0.25)
strategy_specs.edge_requirement: only trade when f* > 0 (equivalently, p > market_price for yes side, or p < market_price for no side)
strategy_specs.true_probability_estimator: NOT SPECIFIED by north star — this is OQ-04. Options: (1) use market-price from another platform as p (crosses into arbitrage), (2) use simple time-decay prior, (3) user-supplied oracle. RECOMMENDED: default to cross-platform mid (p = mean of other-platform prices for the same event) as the baseline estimator; user can override via strategy-parameters.

patterns_found.pattern: f* = (bp - q) / b; fractional Kelly recommended for real-world use
explicit Kelly application to prediction markets with binary 0/1 payoff
simplified form (p - p_m)/(1 - p_m)

pitfalls.description: full-kelly induces ~33% probability of halving bankroll
pitfalls.mitigation: default kelly_fraction = 0.25; expose in strategy-parameters

pitfalls.description: f* can exceed 1 numerically near certainty — would demand >100% of bankroll
pitfalls.mitigation: cap bet_size at strategy-assigned-capital

pitfalls.description: no true probability p means Kelly is meaningless
pitfalls.mitigation: OQ-04 — require strategy-parameters.p_source to be specified at toggle-on time

strategy_specs.detection_rule: For the SAME resolving event present on ≥2 platforms: edge = 1 - (yes_price_platform_A + no_price_platform_B). If edge > min_edge_threshold, submit paired orders: BUY YES on platform A and BUY NO on platform B.
strategy_specs.execution_sequencing_risk: fills are NEVER simultaneous. slippage-model applies independently on each platform. Arbitrage can evaporate mid-execution (one leg fills, other moves). For PAPER TRADING this risk is simulated via: apply slippage-model to leg A first (time t), then re-read platform-B market-price at (t + 30s poll interval or next event), apply slippage-model to leg B. If platform-B price moved adversely past the breakeven, the arb still 'executes' in paper mode but with realised-pnl capturing the loss. This is REALISTIC and prevents trivially profitable synthetic arbs.
strategy_specs.market_matching: cross-platform event identity is OQ-05. Options: (1) manual mapping table, (2) fuzzy match on market-title + market-close-date, (3) hash of normalised event spec. RECOMMENDED: start with manual mapping for the handful of popular cross-listed events (US elections, crypto price markets) and log match candidates for human review.

patterns_found.pattern: edge = 1 - (poly_yes + kalshi_no)
5pp divergence occurs 15-20% of the time; 10pp is high-divergence signal
execution risk dominates — fills are never simultaneous
reference implementation with real-time arb detection (read-only)

pitfalls.description: fee differential can eat the arb — kalshi has up to 3% taker fees
pitfalls.mitigation: min_edge_threshold should account for platform-specific fees; default 2¢ is conservative for low-fee platforms only. OQ-06.

pitfalls.description: market matching errors cause fake arbitrage
pitfalls.mitigation: OQ-05 recommended approach: conservative manual mapping

pitfalls.description: settlement currency mismatch (USDC vs USD vs mana)
pitfalls.mitigation: paper trading abstracts currency to usd; adapter metadata records native currency for real-arb research only

strategy_specs.feature_extraction: within the closing-window, compute momentum_score = (current_market_price - market_price_at_window_open) / window_open_price. Optionally add volume-weighted confirmation: require market-volume-in-window > baseline.
strategy_specs.entry_rule: if momentum_score > +threshold AND current_time within [market-close-date - 6h, market-close-date - exit_buffer]: BUY YES. if momentum_score < -threshold: BUY NO. threshold default 0.05 (5%).
strategy_specs.exit_rule: close at market-resolution, OR at close_buffer before close (default 15 min before) to avoid illiquidity spike, OR on momentum reversal (momentum_score flips sign by more than threshold/2).
strategy_specs.position_size: fixed kelly-fraction or fixed-fraction of strategy-assigned-capital per entry (parameter); scale by recent volume if desired

patterns_found.pattern: momentum = price-change + volume confirmation; exit on momentum weakening
rolling-window momentum with explicit entry/exit thresholds

pitfalls.description: near market-close-date liquidity thins — wide spreads inflate slippage
pitfalls.mitigation: exit_buffer default 15 min; slippage-model's top-of-book walk naturally handles thin books

pitfalls.description: markets can close faster than 6h window if adapter sync missed
pitfalls.mitigation: always check market-close-date - now > 0; skip if negative

strategy_specs.event_detector: within any rolling 15-min window (defaults.news_fade_window_minutes), compute price_move = abs(current_market_price - market_price_15min_ago). If price_move / market_price_15min_ago > 0.10 (defaults.news_fade_trigger_pct = 10%), mark as spike.
strategy_specs.fade_direction: take the opposite side: if spike was UP, BUY NO; if spike was DOWN, BUY YES. Premise is overreaction reverts.
strategy_specs.position_size: fade_fraction (strategy-parameter) * strategy-assigned-capital. Scale inversely with recent volatility to avoid compounding exposure during persistent news cycles.
exit_rules: take-profit: close when price reverts halfway back to pre-spike level (50% retrace)
stop-loss: close if spike continues another 50% in the same direction (i.e. reject the fade)
time-stop: close after 60 minutes regardless (OQ-07 — default configurable)

patterns_found.pattern: wait for overreaction to exhaust, fade the emotion
scale in on further extensions; defined exit levels

pitfalls.description: spikes can be TRUE information — fading them loses money systematically in resolved news
pitfalls.mitigation: max_open_fades parameter caps exposure; stop-loss cuts losers quickly

pitfalls.description: 15-min window detection ambiguous when new tick arrives — rolling vs discrete buckets
pitfalls.mitigation: rolling window over all ticks; re-evaluate on each market-data-event

pitfalls.description: no true news event detection — only price-proxy
pitfalls.mitigation: acceptable; north star explicitly says '10% move in 15min' is the trigger

strategy_specs.quoting_algorithm: Avellaneda-Stoikov-inspired paper MM. Each market-data-event, quote bid = mid - spread/2 + inventory_skew, ask = mid + spread/2 + inventory_skew. Default spread = 2¢ (defaults.market_maker_spread_cents).
strategy_specs.inventory_skew: skew = -gamma * q * sigma^2 * (T - t), where q = current paper-position size, sigma = recent volatility, gamma = risk-aversion parameter. Simplified form: skew = -k * q * bp_per_unit. As long inventory grows, ask tightens and bid loosens, pulling position back to zero.
adverse_selection_protection: widen spread when rolling_volatility > threshold (spike likely means informed flow)
max_inventory parameter: stop quoting one side when |q| ≥ max_inventory
pull quotes within N minutes before market-resolution (stale quote sniper risk)
never quote through the real top-of-book from the adapter — i.e. our simulated bid ≤ real best-bid + slippage budget
strategy_specs.inventory_management: target inventory = 0. Reservation price = mid - q * gamma * sigma^2. Spread maintains profit on mean-reverting flow.

patterns_found.pattern: Avellaneda-Stoikov reservation-price formula + optimal spread
python implementation reference
parameter tuning for gamma, sigma, inventory_target in practical bots

pitfalls.description: in paper trading, our quotes don't interact with real flow — simulating fills requires a counter-flow model
pitfalls.mitigation: OQ-08 — RECOMMENDED: fill MM quote whenever real platform top-of-book CROSSES our quoted price; otherwise, no fill. This matches the slippage-model philosophy of using live prices to adjudicate synthetic orders.

pitfalls.description: T-t (time-to-close) is ambiguous on perpetual manifold markets with no close
pitfalls.mitigation: fallback T-t = 7 days if market-close-date is null

pitfalls.description: gamma parameter tuning is non-trivial
pitfalls.mitigation: expose in strategy-parameters; default gamma = 0.1 and document that positive values increase inventory aversion

open_questions.question: PredictIt has no historical API — backtests will only cover dates since the application first recorded live. How should the UI present this constraint? Should the backtest date-range picker disable dates before the earliest stored-price-history for the selected platform?
open_questions.impact_if_unresolved: person selects 2020 date range for PredictIt, backtest returns empty or misleading results
open_questions.recommended_default: disable date-range dates earlier than min(stored-price-history.timestamp) per platform; show tooltip 'No data recorded before {date}'

open_questions.question: Brier score compares forecast vs outcome. Which price is the forecast — entry price, volume-weighted average of fills, or the market price at moment of resolution?
open_questions.impact_if_unresolved: brier-score tile value shifts materially depending on choice
open_questions.recommended_default: entry price (per paper-fill). Document the choice in tooltip on the brier tile.

open_questions.question: How should paper-positions on markets with market-resolution = cancelled be handled in brier-score, win-rate, P&L?
open_questions.impact_if_unresolved: paper-positions stuck in limbo; metrics numerators/denominators inconsistent
open_questions.recommended_default: refund entry cost (realised-pnl = 0); exclude from brier-score N; exclude from win-rate denominator

open_questions.question: Kelly needs an estimated true probability p. North star does not specify a p-source for kelly-sizing in isolation.
open_questions.recommended_default: strategy-parameter p_source ∈ { 'cross-platform-mean', 'user-override', 'fixed-offset-from-market' }; default = 'cross-platform-mean' (average of market-price across platforms listing the same event, excluding the platform being traded)

open_questions.question: How to determine that a kalshi market and a polymarket market represent the same underlying event?
open_questions.impact_if_unresolved: arbitrage strategy mis-pairs markets → fake arbs with real P&L losses
open_questions.recommended_default: v1: manual mapping table (CSV of kalshi_ticker ↔ polymarket_token_id ↔ manifold_id ↔ predictit_contract_id); v2: fuzzy match on title + close date requiring human confirmation

open_questions.question: Should paper-fills model platform-specific fees (kalshi taker up to 3%, polymarket gas, predictit per-trade)? Stated-intent does not mention fees.
open_questions.recommended_default: add optional fee-model to adapter metadata: 0 by default for strict paper mode; enableable per strategy for realistic sim. OUT OF SCOPE unless person asks.

open_questions.question: News-spike-fade and closing-momentum exits reference time-based cutoffs (60min, 15min pre-close) that are not in defaults list.
open_questions.recommended_default: add to strategy-parameters with defaults (60min fade_timeout, 15min close_buffer); surface in Strategies screen parameter UI

open_questions.question: MM posts passive quotes; the slippage-model in stated-intent only covers AGGRESSIVE orders (mid ± 1¢, top-of-book walk). How do passive quotes get filled in paper mode?
open_questions.impact_if_unresolved: MM strategy never generates paper-fills; cannot demo
open_questions.recommended_default: Passive fill rule: MM quote fills whenever real platform top-of-book CROSSES the quoted price (real best-ask ≤ our bid, or real best-bid ≥ our ask). Fill size = min(quote_size, opposing-side top-of-book size).

open_questions.question: order-side enum includes 'sell'. Can a strategy SHORT a position it doesn't already hold? On real kalshi/polymarket you can — it's equivalent to buying the NO side. Is this the interpretation?
open_questions.impact_if_unresolved: strategies may emit sell orders the engine can't process
open_questions.recommended_default: sell = close existing paper-position (reduce). Opening a short = BUY on the opposite market-side. Surface this in the plugin-interface docstring so strategies follow the convention.

open_questions.question: PredictIt markets can contain multiple contracts; manifold has MULTIPLE_CHOICE and PSEUDO_NUMERIC types. Stated-intent only acknowledges binary.
open_questions.impact_if_unresolved: adapter either drops these markets or ingests them inconsistently
open_questions.recommended_default: v1: filter to binary only. Flatten predictit multi-contract by treating each contract as its own normalised market. Document non-binary support as explicit out-of-scope.
